examples: Tenants are jointly and severally liable for payment of the rent.
Such tort-feasors are jointly and severally liable.
The surviving members of the family came severally every few minutes to the casket and wept above the placid features beneath the glass.
"It often makes you 'joint and severally liable' for any problem, " says Carlton Smith of Cardozo Law School in New York.
He said it was his case that Mr Noble or Mr Lawless or Mr Gibbs "jointly and severally caused the death" of Mr Lavelle.
